title: Portal App Shell (composition)

statusHistory.reason: 9-section homepage live and renders both languages. Composition root src/pages/Home.tsx imports all section components. Routing in App.tsx exposes /, /en, /intake, /tier-0, /demo/sunday-night-dread, /showcase/:slug, /engagement/:slug — all serving 200. Active criteria met (analysis, implementation, decisions, successCriteria all populated).

problem: Layout, navigation, theming, language switching, and global state composing the 7 portal primitives into Marc's specific portal experience. Marc-specific — this is the only feature in the project that is NOT a reusable primitive pattern. Bilingual (French primary, English secondary per Quebec context). Static-first wherever possible. Hosted on Marc's own paid tier. Includes the public homepage with the 9-section architecture from the brainstorming session: hero, how-it-works (4 steps), demo session embed, showcases wall, self-qualify section (vibe-filter), pricing transparency, CTA, about Marc, footer. The composition layer is where Marc-specific branding and copy lives — primitives stay generic.
analysis: The composition layer where the 7 generic primitives + the demo content are wired into Marc's specific portal experience. NOT a reusable primitive — this is where Marc-specific copy, branding, language choices, and visual identity live. Includes the public homepage with the 9-section architecture from Phase 2 of the brainstorming session: hero (name, photo, 'dev à Québec, j'ai une job de jour'), how-it-works (4-step process with 72h SLA visible), demo session embed (Sunday Night Dread), showcases wall, vibe-filter section ('we do / we don't'), pricing transparency, CTA ('crée ton compte gratuit'), about Marc, footer. Bilingual French primary / English secondary. Static-first wherever possible.

decisions.rationale: Insight #18 + Quebec context. The Facebook post (Draft D from Phase 2) is French with 'aussi disponible en anglais' bilingual marker. Same logic at the page level.
decisions.recommendation: French content lives at /, English mirror at /en. Language switcher in nav. Showcase pages can be in client's primary language. Compliance with OQLF / French language laws is a hard requirement for commercial sites in Quebec.

decisions.decision: 9-section homepage architecture (hero / how-it-works / demo / showcases / vibe-filter / pricing / CTA / about / footer)
decisions.rationale: Phase 2 brainstorming established this as the expectation-coherent ordering. Each section answers a specific visitor question in sequence.
decisions.recommendation: Build sections in the order they appear, but build the demo section first (highest leverage per Insight #22). The page is incomplete without the demo session embedded.

decisions.rationale: Boring-tech mandate (Insight #35 + F2 defenses). Static = cheap, fast, indexable, low-maintenance. Dynamic features (account, intake form, engagement canvas) live in subroutes that can be SPA or server-rendered.
decisions.recommendation: Static homepage + showcase pages via Cloudflare Pages or similar Canada-friendly host. Account-gated areas (intake, engagement canvas) on a separate dynamic subdomain or path. Total monthly hosting cost <$50.

successCriteria:
- Public homepage renders all 9 sections with French primary content
- English mirror at /en with structurally identical layout
- Demo session embed loads and is interactive (live preview iframe of feat-demo-sunday-night-dread)
- Static pages indexable by Google with proper Quebec-local SEO meta
- All 7 primitives + demo composed visibly into the portal experience
- Hosting in Canada (Loi 25 alignment), monthly cost <$50
- NOT a reusable primitive — this composition is Marc-specific by design
implementation: src/pages/Home.tsx is the composition root — it imports each section component (Hero, HowItWorks, DemoEmbed, ShowcasesWall, VibeFilter, Pricing, CTA, About, Footer) and a Header with a language switcher. App.tsx wires routing: '/' → <Home lang='fr' />, '/en' → <Home lang='en' />, '*' → redirect to '/'. All copy lives in src/i18n.ts as a single DICT keyed by Lang ('fr' | 'en'); every section reads from DICT[lang]. Design tokens are CSS custom properties in src/styles.css (values copied from @majeanson/lac-react warm-amber palette, not imported). Capacity counter (CapacityCounter.tsx) currently uses static copy from i18n; will fetch from feat-triage-queue once that primitive exists. No build-time prerendering yet — SPA renders client-side. SEO meta + <html lang> updated in a useEffect when the page mounts.
userGuide: Local development: `cd portal && npm install && npm run dev` opens at http://localhost:5180. FR primary at '/', EN at '/en'. Edit copy in src/i18n.ts (one dictionary, both languages). Edit visual rules in src/styles.css (CSS custom properties at the top). Add a section: create a component in src/components/, import it in src/pages/Home.tsx, add a translation key set in i18n.ts. Add a route: edit src/App.tsx. Production build: `npm run build` (typechecks, then Vite builds to dist/). Hosting and deploy pipeline are not yet configured — current artifact is a local-dev build only.
knownLimitations: Client-side rendered SPA — Google can index but initial paint requires JS; consider prerender/SSG before launch if SEO matters earlier than the showcase wall builds gravity
Capacity counter shows static copy from i18n.ts; not wired to a real triage queue source
Showcase entries are hardcoded in src/components/ShowcasesWall.tsx — no rendering from feature.json files yet (case-study-renderer primitive not built)
No /showcase/:slug route yet — links from DemoEmbed and ShowcasesWall 404 (will redirect to '/')
No build-time language detection or hreflang tags — relies on the language switcher and document.documentElement.lang set in useEffect
Hosting target not chosen — _redirects/vercel.json not yet configured for SPA deep-link routing

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — composition wires the 7 primitives into Marc's specific portal (Phase 2 product page architecture, Theme 4 insights).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: 9-section homepage built. Composition root at src/pages/Home.tsx; copy in src/i18n.ts; routing in src/App.tsx. Listed concrete known gaps (no /showcase route, hardcoded showcase entries, capacity counter not wired) so they don't get lost when this primitive is reviewed for active/frozen advancement.
